Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Don Willenburg of GORDON & REES LLP (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party Hennessy Industries, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Raymond Cronin, et al. (#274286), Arvin Reece, et al. (#274951), Noel Shusted, et al. (#275378), Ted V. Kennedy, et al. (#275392)

RULING:
DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN RAYMOND CRONIN (#274286), ARVIN REECE, (#274951), NOEL SHUSTED (#275378), TED V. KENNEDY (#275392) CASES

Prior to the hearing both counsel stipulated that the same argument could be applied to all four cases: Raymond Cronin (#274286), Arvin Reece, (#274951), Noel Shusted (#275378), Ted V. Kennedy (#275392)
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for decedent's alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant's Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to decedent. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. Orders for all four cases were signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Sarnoff Court Reporters, (415) 274-9977

APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff's counsel Chris Hersom and Jennifer Alesio of Brayton Purcell LLP.
Honeywell counsel David Ongaro of Ongaro, Burtt & Louderback.

At 2:30 p.m., counsel informs the court that Union Carbide has settled out of the Walker case. The court consolidates the Walker case with Group 1 for trial.

The plaintiff requests that the Utterback case be consolidated as well because the cancer allegation is being dropped from the complaint. After hearing argument, the court takes the matter under submission.

Pertinent pre-hardship motions in limine for the cases are discussed by court and counsel. The court will decide whether or not to consolidate Utterback on Tuesday morning (10/11/11). Jury hardshipping will be conducted that day in the afternoon.

The court takes a recess from 3:11 p.m. to 3:15 p.m.

The court will need a generic statement of the case from counsel on Tuesday morning. Counsel will not need to be present for jury hardshipping. The motion to consolidate Utterback will be served on defendants in Groups 1 and 2 as well as the Utterback defense counsel.

The cases will reconvene on Tuesday, October 11, 2011 at 10:00 a.m. for pre-hardship motions in limine, the consolidation ruling on Utterback and the generic statement of the case. Jury hardshipping will commence on that day at 1:30 p.m. Voir dire will take place on Wednesday, 10/12/11, at 9:30 a.m.

At 3:30 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

Defendant Volkswagen Group Of America¿S Motion For Recovery Of Expert Costs Pursuant To Ccp 998
Off Calendar Apr-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Garrett Sanderson III, Esq., of CARROLL, BURDICK & McDONOUGH LLP (415-989-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party Volkswagen Group of America
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Raymond Cronin, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR RECOVERY OF EXPERT COSTS PURSUANT TO CCP 998
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Volkswagen Group of America's motion for recovery of expert is granted. Defendant is awarded $91,697.42 in expert costs. The inclusion of release language in the 998 offers does not render the offers invalid. The offers were reasonable when made considering plaintiffs' responses to defendant's specially-prepared discovery. While the amount of the expert costs is quite high, the costs were reasonable in amount and reasonably necessary to defendant's defense of plaintiff's claims. Perhaps the most significant issue is whether defendant's decision to file its summary judgment motion close to trial (and thus the court's ruling was not rendered until after defendant's incurrence of almost $100,000 in expert costs) is a basis to deny this motion in whole or in part. The court is unaware of any authority directly on point and believes that, since defendant would likely have been entitled to these costs had it prevailed at trial whether or not it had filed a motion for summary judgment, the timing of the motion for summary judgment is not a basis to deny any of the claimed expert costs. Counsel for defendant is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Per March 30,2011 Hearing)
Off Calendar Apr-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Paul Lannus, Esq., of LANKFORD CRAWFORD MORENO LLP (925-300-3520), appearing for defendant / moving party Ford Motor Company
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Shirley Cronin, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (PER MARCH 30,2011 HEARING)
Prior to the hearing the Court stated on the record approving Ford's motion paper filed on 12/15/2010 as moving paper properly filed on behalf of this motion. The Court further deemed plaintiffs' opposition filed as Exhibits G-K to the Elizabeth Leonard's declaration in support of plaintiffs' motion to vacate filed on 3/2/2011 as opposition to this motion and properly filed.
Ford's motion for summary judgment was argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and granted as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages). The declaration of Mr. Aahl, the deposition testimony of Mr. Rocker and Mr. Taylor and defendant's responses to GO 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Cronin was exposed to asbestos-containing brake and clutch parts attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Mr. Aahl are overruled. While the excerpts of Mr. Aahl's deposition testimony provide significant impeachment of his statements in his declaration, the deposition testimony does not show that Mr. Aahl lacks personal knowledge for the statements he made in his declaration. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for false representation and punitive damages. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
